title: Design Principles for Inflammasome Inhibition by Pyrin-Only-Proteins
abstract: Inflammasomes are filamentous signaling platforms essential for host defense against various intracellular calamities such as pathogen invasion and genotoxic stresses. However, dysregulated inflammasomes cause an array of human diseases including autoinflammatory disorders and cancer. It was recently identified that endogenous pyrin-only-proteins (POPs) regulate inflammasomes by directly inhibiting their filament assembly. Here, by combining Rosetta in silico, in vitro, and in cellulo methods, we investigate the target specificity and inhibition mechanisms of POPs. In contrast to a previous report, we find that POP1 is a poor inhibitor of the central inflammasome adaptor ASC. Instead, POP1 inhibits the assembly of upstream receptor PYD filaments such as those of AIM2, IFI16, NLRP3, and NLRP6. Moreover, not only does POP2 directly suppress the nucleation of ASC, but it can also inhibit the elongation of receptor filaments. In addition to inhibiting the elongation of AIM2 and NLRP6 filam
authors: Mazanek, Z.; Wu, S.; Belotte, G.; Zhou, J. J.; Stallings, C. M.; Garg, A.; Lueck, J.; Sohn, J.
key_results: In contrast to a previous report, we find that POP1 is a poor inhibitor of the central inflammasome adaptor ASC
Instead, POP1 inhibits the assembly of upstream receptor PYD filaments such as those of AIM2, IFI16, NLRP3, and NLRP6
Moreover, not only does POP2 directly suppress the nucleation of ASC, but it can also inhibit the elongation of receptor filaments
In addition to inhibiting the elongation of AIM2 and NLRP6 filaments, POP3 potently suppresses the nucleation of ASC
